chapter_title.en: Chapter on Oaths, Vows, and Expiations

text: 4335 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "كَفَّارَاتُ اَلْمَجَالِسِ أَنْ تَقُولَ عِنْدَ قِيَامِكَ مِنْهَا: ۝ سُبْحٰانَ رَبِّكَ رَبِّ اَلْعِزَّةِ عَمّٰا يَصِفُونَ وَ سَلاٰمٌ عَلَى اَلْمُرْسَلِينَ وَ اَلْحَمْدُ لِلّٰهِ رَبِّ اَلْعٰالَمِينَ ۝ "

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.4335 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"The expiation for gatherings is to say upon leaving them: 'Glory be to your Lord (azj), the Lord (azj) of Honor, above what they describe. And peace be upon the messengers. And all praise is due to Allah (swt), the Lord (azj) of the worlds.' (Surah As-Saffat 37:180-182)."
